title: What is Competitive Landscape of Joby Aviation Company?
text: The competitive landscape shows a hierarchical feature with Joby Aviation occupying the leading position among eVTOL companies. Joby ended the quarter with $2.5 billion in cash and short-term investments, giving it the strongest cash position among major eVTOL companies. Key competitors include Archer Aviation (ACHR), which has a partnership with United Airlines, and Lilium, which focuses on a unique jet-fan propulsion system. Archer follows closely behind, forming the first echelon. EHang has achieved global breakthroughs in airworthiness certification and commercial implementation. Enterprises such as Vertical Aerospace and Eve Holding form the second echelon. Having completed the third of five stages of the Systems Review and commenced for-credit FAA conformity testing for its propulsion battery in early 2024, Joby is among the frontrunners in achieving commercial operational readiness. Through in-depth cooperation with Toyota and Delta Air Lines, JOBY has built high barriers in manufacturing and market channels. The global eVTOL market is projected to expand from $0.76 billion in 2024 to $4.67 billion by 2030, advancing at a compound annual growth rate of 35.3 percent. Established aerospace giants such as Embraer and Airbus are increasing their investments and expanding their presence in the sector.
